steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: The grammatical subject is "Barrada's pieces" — a plural noun. The long participial phrase ("utilizing elements as disparate as plant-dyed fabrics, wire crab traps filled with stones, and cotton balls dangling above a fan") is a modifier that separates the subject from the blank but does not change it. A finite, plural main verb is required.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'has explored': Singular present-perfect verb; violates subject-verb agreement with the plural subject "Barrada's pieces." ✗
  • Choice B — 'explore': Simple present plural verb; agrees with the plural subject "Barrada's pieces." ✓
  • Choice C — 'explores': Singular present verb; violates subject-verb agreement with the plural subject. ✗
  • Choice D — 'exploring': A present participle, not a finite verb; a sentence cannot be complete without a finite main verb, so this creates a fragment. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
